GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "How Barbie is helping countless girls make their career dreams come true", "The empowering doll brand's Dream Gap Project is dispelling one negative gender perception after another.", "While the childhood hobby of doll play may have once been overlooked as nothing more than a few moments of lighthearted make believe, recent years have seen this perception turned on its head. Having already shown that doll play may aid a child's development of empathy and social processing skill, the act of playing with dolls may also be to thank for positively impacting an individual's future career ambitions. Research has shown that girls begin to internalise limiting self-beliefs from the age of five, this negative outlook in turn seeing girls look upon their gender, and in turn their abilities, through a narrow scope. Having long been fed social and culture stereotypes through the media and those around them, be it covertly or overtly, girls have been taught to feel inferior to men, whether it's physically or mentally, this period when their confidence begins to crack has been dubbed the Dream Gap–and that's where the Barbie Dream Gap Project comes in. Since 2018, it's been Barbie's brand mission to give girls across the globe the resources and support they need to transcend the Dream Gap so they may continue to believe that they can be anything. In fact, Barbie's mission to inspire limitless potential in every girl through the encouragement of imagination, expression, and discovery, through play, has existed since the doll brand's inception in 1959. Through the Barbie Dream Gap Project, the empowering doll brand is working to tackle the self-doubt and discouragement to pursue paths that value brilliance and stop the negative impact of girls' self-limiting beliefs. Aiding these girls to rediscover their purpose and untapped potential, the global Barbie Dream Gap Project works to highlight positive role models through inspiring content and products. Think Barbie's career line–the offering produced more than 200 dolls and counting, representing a number of professional paths, from princess and president, to astronaut to zoologist–which has highlighted careers where women are unrepresented, or its Inspiring Women collection, which showcases historical female figures who have broken barriers, such as Rosa Parks and Sally Ride. And even its Shero Program, which showcases remarkable women who are breaking boundaries, and inspiring the next generation, through a one-of-a-kind doll made in their likeness, each of these ranges allowing girls to actually see that if these women can reach their own sky-high goals, they themselves can do anything. Read more about the Barbie Dream Gap Project here, and help girls everywhere to #CloseTheDreamGap."
